deviations.description: The process model on page 1 shows that after obtaining patient consent, the nurse performs the time out procedure. Version 2 now requires the doctor to perform the time out procedure. The process does not assign this activity to the doctor, resulting in a violation of the new requirement.

Version 2 requires that after obtaining patient consent, the next activity must be the time out procedure. The process model allows for other activities or branching before the time out procedure, which violates the stricter sequential requirement.
process_model_reference: sequence between obtain patient consent and time out procedure

deviations.description: The requirement that the nurse must obtain patient consent is already enforced in the process model, and the resource assignment does not change from Version 1 to Version 2.
The process model enforces that the nurse performs the time out procedure, which is no longer required in Version 2. This results in unnecessary restriction beyond the updated requirement.
process_model_reference: time out procedure performed by nurse
